arguments.task: Research Clay using official and first-party sources only. Start at https://clay.com. Find Clay's official developer or API documentation, authentication documentation, pricing/access information, any changelog or support docs about API availability, and any official or first-party page about MCP or model context protocol. Also check Clay's official GitHub organization or first-party docs site if linked from the website. Extract: one-line product description, API availability and surface type (REST/GraphQL/none), auth methods for API, whether access is self-serve free/trial/paid/admin/partner gated, rate limit notes, and whether an MCP server exists. Return concise findings with exact source URLs from official pages only.

input.thought: Start browser research on Clay official sources.

raw_result.description: Data enrichment and outbound workflow platform for building tables, automations, and personalized go-to-market workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official evidence gathered indicates webhook endpoints and an Enterprise-only native Clay API, but public docs reviewed do not verify a specific documented API auth scheme such as OAuth2, API key, basic, or bearer token. MCP is enabled through Clay workspace setup. Marked as other pending human verification of the Enterprise API auth method.
raw_result.gating_reason: Clay pricing indicates native 'Clay API access' is only available on the Enterprise plan, while self-serve tiers expose platform features, webhook automation, and HTTP API integrations for calling external APIs rather than a general public Clay API.

api_surface.breadth: No traditional public general-purpose Clay REST or GraphQL API was verified from the gathered first-party evidence. Official docs instead describe webhook ingestion plus a limited Enterprise-only People and Company API for basic lookups, and official MCP support.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Clay has an official MCP page describing use with ChatGPT, Codex, and Claude.

raw_result.blocker: No verified self-serve public API for Clay was found; native Clay API access appears Enterprise-gated/contact-sales only, and the public evidence does not document enough auth and endpoint detail to build a general toolkit today.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No public numeric API rate limits were verified in the gathered evidence. The reviewed Clay documentation reportedly notes Clay is not intended for millisecond-response request patterns, but exact limits were not confirmed from visible page text.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official pricing distinguishes 'HTTP API integrations' and webhook automation from separate Enterprise 'Clay API access'. Official MCP support exists. Official GitHub organization exists, but gathered evidence did not show an active public API reference suitable for immediate toolkit construction.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Key fields remain ambiguous from the gathered evidence: the exact authentication method for the Enterprise Clay API was not publicly verified, and the 'using-clay-as-an-api' page content was inferred through browser task extraction rather than directly inspectable text in-chat. API surface classification is therefore conservative.

result.description: Data enrichment and outbound workflow platform for building tables, automations, and personalized go-to-market workflows.
result.auth_notes: Official evidence gathered indicates webhook endpoints and an Enterprise-only native Clay API, but public docs reviewed do not verify a specific documented API auth scheme such as OAuth2, API key, basic, or bearer token. MCP is enabled through Clay workspace setup. Marked as other pending human verification of the Enterprise API auth method.
result.gating_reason: Clay pricing indicates native 'Clay API access' is only available on the Enterprise plan, while self-serve tiers expose platform features, webhook automation, and HTTP API integrations for calling external APIs rather than a general public Clay API.

api_surface.mcp_notes: Clay has an official MCP page describing use with ChatGPT, Codex, and Claude.

result.notable_signals: Official pricing distinguishes 'HTTP API integrations' and webhook automation from separate Enterprise 'Clay API access'. Official MCP support exists. Official GitHub organization exists, but gathered evidence did not show an active public API reference suitable for immediate toolkit construction.
